comment: too much homework for a cms class. the professor locks all the assignments too so you can't work ahead and speedrun the assignments. definitely avoid
Professor got his PhD in something irrelevant so he has a superiority complex

the creative assignments are ridiculous, I took a 0 on one of them because I legit didn't have enough friends to do it. Professor also told off a dude who was older than him just bc his phone went off, he needs to chillax

Although an "easy 4.0", this class has been nothing short of miserable. Mandatory lectures that basically regurgitates information introduced in the mandatory several hour pre-recorded lectures. Weekly creative assignments that are tedious. Just a ridiculous set of standards; don't waste your time.

Not a super hard class, but insanely tedious. Lots of lectures, lots of assignments, and the professor seems to believe that this is the only class students should care about. Way too much work for a 200 level class, but the only actual hard things are the creative assignments cuz he assumes you have access to editing software (and friends).
not worth the effort unless you really care about chinese movies. prof is full of himself too
Absolute joke lol stay as far away as possible. Talk about a guy with a huge ego who needs a reality check.
way too much for a 200-level film class, flipped classroom. pls avoid.
this teacher is so bad,with bad explanationt and too much homework

I cannot express how ridiculous this class is within RMP's 350 letter count, but all I can say is while it is an "easy A," there is a LOT of tedious work. 2 hour lectures could easily be condensed to 45 minutes, and there are additional 2+ hour online lectures to watch in order to complete homework assignments. AI will be your friend for this class

This class is a complete joke. There is an obscene amount of work. The lecture is 2 hours, which doesn't seem so bad until you realize that you just had to spend 2-3 hours watching the pre recorded lectures and writing about them. You'll also receive class wide emails about how you are a bad citizen for using AI because it kills the environment.

An unbelievable amount of pointless work. Before attending the near 2 hour long lecture, an additional 2-3 hours of pre recorded lectures must be watched beforehand. Professor clearly cares about the subject material, though I find this is often translated into pretentiousness and an annoyance with the amount of irrelevant canvas messages sent out.

Do not take this class. Changed how the class is. Long assignments, all of which are completely pointless. You have to do "creative assignments" and make videos and photos relating to the movies. Flipped class, so you have to watch hours of lectures to then go hear the exact same stuff in class. Avoid.

This class is a mess. Way too much work. Not only are we told to go outside to capture content but expected to read around 50 pages of texts and provide 300 word explanations for each class (2x a week). We also have 3 creative assignments which requires us to write 1,000 words. Takes more effort than all my other classes combined

Professor Braester is very knowledgeable and passionate about the material he teaches; his lectures are detailed, informative, and thought provoking. The films chosen for the coursework are varied and intriguing. The assignment prompts are well thought out and he is always available for questions or encouragement. Beware group projects!

The lectures were boring, but I enjoyed the quiz sections so much. The course is mainly about watching Chinese films and later writing a paragraph that was self-graded. If you need an easy A&H class take CMS&271

class is self graded so easy A! i recommend going to class though because he's very sweet and tries very hard to get students engaged. lots of movies to watch but they were honestly entertaining af and i loved them. easy class but lecture heavy so be warned ! sometimes i'd have two hour lecture in class and two hours to watch for homework lol
It's insanely lecture-heavy, and I think you just have to be incredibly passionate about/interested in the specific topic to enjoy the class, even if you're interested in film.

There was one specific film that I was particularly eager to see, which was not included in the syllabus. Upon sharing my interest with Yomi, he kindly provided me access to the film so that I could watch it. Class was largely lecture-based but engaging due to his passion. Assignments were relatively brief and enjoyable to complete.
He is the best prof at UW. I took three of his courses. He always gave us honest feedback and suggestions. His classes were engaging and very interesting!

Yomi possesses an impressive depth of knowledge, particularly in the realm of world cinema. Although the weekly readings can be challenging, his patience and willingness to assist students make the learning experience enjoyable. I thoroughly appreciated the class!

Professor Braester is a kind and caring person who is very passionate about his work. His lectures can be slow, but the course load is very manageable, and we had no exams. This is a good class to take if you want to balance out your schedule.

Yomi is so gentle and kind, and very passionate about genre films. The class is extremely lecture/film/essay heavy, and has 3 group projects per quarter. But it's very easy, and you don't really need to watch the movies to answer prompts, just be prepared for 6-7 hours a week of lecture alone, not to mention the 2 weekly films. Can afford to skip.

His course work is not difficult but takes a little time. We need to complete both the pre-recorded lecture and the offline lecture. He respects the culture and the classroom, and the film clips and cases he shows are interesting. He started using the non-grading system, let the students control their own grades. I would recommend taking his class.

Frighteningly slow lectures. Even at 2x, a snail coming home from a funeral would have more energy than him. The entirety of his 2 hour gathering could be explained in approximately 3 slides on a PowerPoint which he enjoys dragging out, and is oblivious to how he behaves. Quiz sections could be condensed to 1 slide and TAs still drag it out. YAWN!!

I really enjoyed Professor Braester's class, which he taught collaboratively with Prof. Chris Hamm. They had organized presentations and engaged students. At the end of the quarter, they brought in the director and producer of one of the films we'd watched. A really great experience if you are looking to dive into CMS.

The overall class and writing assignments are easy, but the grading rubric is stupid. Clarity? Adherence to Prompt? Depending on the TA, it's hard to get full points because of his subjective grading rubric. My TA always nitpicked and took off points. This is how he maintains a stupid 3.3 median. With artificial difficulty in a film studies class.

Yomi is absolutely a wealth of knowledge on Chinese cinema. If you have a chance to take his class online (and watch him speak on 2x on video), do so - he speaks very slowly. You will be exposed to a trove of great cinematic analysis, cultural commentary, and a very bright mind. One of the best classes I've taken at the UW.

Coming from a non-major. No attendance. No live lectures. No exams. Don't have to do the readings or even watch the lectures to complete most of the assignments. Two weekly 250 word essays. Surprisingly tough grading, picky TA. BS essay prompts where you have to stretch and find deep meanings in surface level stuff, busy work. Overall easy tho.

This class was okay. I enjoyed the content and felt like the films we watched were quite interesting but at some times, the classes dragged on. He talks a lot and quite slowly but overall is not a hard grader. If you show up to class and put in the work, it won't be hard to do well.

Yomi gets somewhat of a bad rep from non Cinema Studies students at UW, but the guy has changed my life for the better. After my 3rd CMS class with Yomi, I realized what an unbelievable wealth of knowledge he is. Never a bad question with him, and my guy always adds insights. Be prepared to work, yet know that Yomi is a worldwide legend.

The entire class was based off of papers on movies and readings that were done outside of class and the papers were always around 500 words so only like a page and a half. Not bad at all, and you really never had to go to class and could do great. I skipped at least once a week and just did the assignments and always got 100% on them.

Yomi is super passionate, and while he can get on a tangent easily and spend a whole class on it, he loves teaching and is very accessible and easy to talk to and understanding. He knows a ton about film

the class material is fun and easy, but he made it very hard to understand, he always stuck in a question and keep asking student to answer. he never reply email or any question posted online. worst thing is he fail to open the video he want to show in class every time for the whole quarter. and he always takes more than 5 mins to fix that...

This Professor drags lecture material that could fit in a 50 min class into a 2 hour lecture. The material is boring and useless, he doesn't approach the material in a exciting matter but in lifeless one. Don't take this class for an easy grade, you will regret it.

I think because of the class I took I had a positive experience with Yomi. He didn't seem annoying to me, he even used memes in his lectures. MEMES. He tried to crack jokes? But the class was also jointly taught with Chris Hamm. He is quite hard to follow, and does go in a circle of rants but it came off more as passionate to me than arrogant.
He's arrogant, but without a reason to be. He's like the poster boy for those attacking academia as elitist and useless-- he's almost a perfect caricature in this respect.

Prof. Braester is an very strange person, but not "elitist" as others say. If he behaved the way he does AND was at Harvard AND had Harvard-level intellect, then he'd be elitist. No, he just seems to have an inferiority complex where he needs to prove how smart he thinks he is & put others down to pump himself up.

He's a nice professor that knows his material well. He tries hard to make sure the students take part by pointing at students and asking them questions, which is sometimes scary if you don't know the answer. He has that philosophical attitude, wanting to dive deeper even though its a simple concept. He hates students using their phone in class.

His lectures are super slow and annoying because it takes him an hour to explain a 10 minute concept. Can't space out during class either because he and his TA's like to randomly pick on people in class so I would do the readings and skip lecture. The whole class is on one Chinese director, which means a lot of subtitles and hard names.

As a film major I found this class to be incredibly easy and sometimes boring, but I did learn a couple things about global cinema and watched many new movies. Lectures can feel long but the final assignments are all very easy! I really enjoyed the SIFF aspect of the course, it was a good excuse to go to many films at the festival.
Decent professor, interesting material. Gets upset when people use their phones in class though, but oh well.

I really enjoyed this class! It was a lot of fun to talk about films in intricate detail with Yomi and the other dedicated students. For this class you'll get weekly readings, movies to watch, and quickly answer a couple questions plus 3 main essays. A pleasure taking his class and work is doable and worth it!

He is a thoughtful intelligent professor who knows his stuff and gives good feedback. Sometimes he got criticized for not being that clear with essay expectations, but he really tries to clarify. In the end, everything came together and worked.
confusing, condescending

Yomi is interesting to listen to and his class is an easy high 3.0/4.0 as long as you attend the lectures and screenings. Grade consists of 3 essays (500, 1000, and 1500 word), attendance in quiz section, and scores on post lecture quizzes(fairly easy).
Yomi is a good professor! The class contents are interesting as well. There are many movies to watch. If you want to get VLPA, this class is a good choice to go.

Most of the films are interesting, but he shows some very boring&slow ones too. Lectures are hard to understand and extremely boring because his analyses/interpretations are so deep and longwinded. But the workload for this class is also manageable
He is a nice guy who is crazy about Zhang Yi Mou. If you are into Chinese cinema, you should take C LIT 271 or 272 with him. Weekly quizzes and a final paper.

He is pretty awesome, and he presents really interesting topics and interpretations that make otherwise terribly boring films worth watching again...I dont have much of an interest in Chinese film but after him, i'd be down to watch the in class screenings again just see his interpretations and views. He's very talky but interesting with enough cof
Yomi is a very intelligent guy but extremely boring. The movies that we watched were almost all horrible. Do not take this class

He definitely knows his stuff.. Lectures aren't too bad, but he has the WEIRDEST way of wording things. I had to re-read his lectures notes at least twice to know what he's really trying to say. The movies that he shows, however, gets progressively worse... But he's pretty easy and a reasonable guy. I actually recommend him.

Yomi was a great professor. There were daily quizzes, but all open note (final was open note too). He knows what he is talking about and makes it very easy to get a good grade in the class. I highly recommend him!
Most boring professor i have had so far in my college career and probably wont top him. He knows his chinese films but picks the worst movies to watch. P.S. dont take this class

The guy really knows his chinese films and is a decent lecturer, but is somewhat boring. However, he seems to pick the WORST films to watch possible. If you are taking the class so you can watch movies, this is a mistake. Most of the Chinese movies we've watched are awful and incredibly dull. Skip the class if possible.

He knows his stuff but that isn't enough to qualify him as a good professor. Insanely boring lectures! Like half the class sleeping or on facebook boring. Also far too full of himself which holds him back from being likeable.

interesting accent from Israel, however, lectures are boring... don't get me wrong, the guy knows his stuff, studied at the top university in Israel, got his masters at Yale, and his P.H.D. at Berkley but... he acts like an elite prick sometimes and its hard to understand his termanology. Also he makes you take daily tests...
Professor Braester is hilarious. His film analysis was visually very interesting and you could tell he really cared about the subject.

His israeli accent makes his dialogue very interesting to listen to. His slideshows are very professionally made and his lectures are quite intriguing. However, the concept of the information is a bit difficult to grasp. All in all, a great professor.

Really smart teacher, but acts kind of elitist. He'll use his own translations of literature in class if he deems the published translation is not good enough. YOU MUST READ A LOT IN THIS CLASS, OR YOU WILL FAIL. Daily graded writing, so attendance is a must. Not a class for the lazy or weak-hearted.
Sep 15th, 2005

Good class however Yomi said some of the most pointless things ever.Obvious he knew his stuff-but a sense of elitism surrounded his lessons making them a dread to listen to.Also a really tough grader and is known for curving DOWN to keep courses from being
this guy was wicked sweet. and he really knew his stuff. i'd definitely reccommend him. my friends & i tried to break down his accent all quarter. good stuff.
This class made me HATE martial arts films. Which is hard to do. Yomi made me want to gouge my eyes out everytime he spoke.

Testimony and Allegory class. This class is hard. If your'e stupid or lazy do not take this class. Yomi is brilliant, check out his CV if you have doubts. Take it, you'll get an Ivy league course for state prices, (I'm at Columbia now, this class was good prep.)
He's obviously extremely intelligent, and very passionate about what he teaches. I think he's definitely underappreciated by most students.
Dec 8th, 2004
He's not the best teacher ever but he means well and film is always worth taking.
Mar 1st, 2004
Beware The Opium Wars. I think this class COULD have been interesting, it just. . . wasn't.
Teaches the most boring Chinese film class in the history of mankind.
